findings.reason: The legacy RISC-V prover still selects a concrete CPU backend instead of depending on the backend-neutral prover contract.
findings.next_extraction: Move backend selection to the RISC-V tool boundary and leave the frontend dependent only on backend-neutral proof interfaces.

findings.reason: The legacy Metal shader monolith is retained only for the staged, ABI-preserving library migration.
findings.next_extraction: Move the next kernel family into the linked shader library without adding a runtime library or dispatch boundary.

findings.reason: The Objective-C Metal runtime still combines several ABI and device-resource families behind one implementation unit.
findings.next_extraction: Extract the next cohesive ABI implementation family behind the existing stable headers and resource ownership contracts.

findings.reason: The Zig Metal runtime facade still owns legacy dispatch and orchestration responsibilities that have not reached focused modules.
findings.next_extraction: Move the next independently testable runtime responsibility behind the existing facade without changing its public ABI.

findings.reason: The root build graph declares the repository's full executable, test, vector, and conformance surface in one file.
findings.next_extraction: Move one cohesive build-product family into build support with explicit inputs while keeping build.zig as the visible graph root.

findings.reason: The RISC-V trace-column module retains legacy AIR layout and construction responsibilities in one file.
findings.next_extraction: Separate the next stable trace-layout responsibility from trace population after shared Cairo and prover boundaries settle.

findings.reason: The RISC-V infrastructure trace retains multiple legacy construction stages in one module.
findings.next_extraction: Extract the next independently specified infrastructure table with component-level tests in the RISC-V migration phase.

findings.reason: The legacy RISC-V prover combines frontend planning, backend selection, and proof orchestration.
findings.next_extraction: Move concrete backend selection out first, then split frontend planning from backend-neutral proof orchestration.

findings.reason: The RISC-V runner trace module retains several legacy execution-to-witness transformations.
findings.next_extraction: Separate the next witness table transformation behind an explicit runner trace contract.

findings.reason: The Cairo-to-Metal facade still contains legacy proving-stage bindings not yet moved to their resident owners.
findings.next_extraction: Move the next complete proving stage under integrations/cairo_metal/resident while preserving facade names and protocol order.

findings.reason: The compatibility facade still contains legacy arena-plan command and diagnostic responsibilities.
findings.next_extraction: Move the next command family into src/tools/metal_arena_plan and reduce the facade without adding policy to the arena.

findings.reason: Benchmark parsing, matching, statistical comparison, and report publication are still combined in one maintained script.
findings.next_extraction: Move the stable result model and comparison engine into a focused benchmark_delta package, leaving the script as CLI orchestration.

findings.reason: Optimization evidence discovery, validation, comparison, and presentation remain in one maintained script.
findings.next_extraction: Separate evidence validation and comparison from command-line presentation using the existing benchmark report contracts.

findings.reason: SN PIE schedule construction, validation, serialization, and CLI policy remain combined in one maintained script.
findings.next_extraction: Move the validated schedule model and pure construction stages into a package, retaining a thin artifact-producing CLI.

findings.reason: Queue configuration, process control, proof publication, and sustained-run reporting remain combined in one maintained script.
findings.next_extraction: Separate the deterministic queue model and proof-result publication from process lifecycle orchestration.

findings.reason: Persistent Metal session lifecycle, protocol I/O, artifact policy, and reporting remain combined in one maintained script.
findings.next_extraction: Move session protocol types and artifact validation into focused modules while keeping one lifecycle owner.

findings.reason: The native proof matrix regression suite covers several evidence and controller responsibilities in one legacy test file.
findings.next_extraction: Mirror the native_proof_matrix_lib ownership modules with focused test files while retaining end-to-end contract coverage.

findings.reason: The SN PIE queue test suite combines model, lifecycle, failure, and publication scenarios in one legacy file.
findings.next_extraction: Mirror the queue responsibilities as they are extracted, preserving shared fixtures and end-to-end streaming coverage.

findings.reason: The persistent-session test suite combines protocol, cache, lifecycle, and failure scenarios in one legacy file.
findings.next_extraction: Mirror the session protocol and lifecycle modules with focused tests while retaining end-to-end restart coverage.

findings.reason: The legacy Metal backend test graph combines many independent runtime and kernel families in one file.
findings.next_extraction: Move the next kernel or runtime family into a mirrored focused test module without reducing shared integration coverage.

findings.reason: The Rust trace-dump tool combines artifact decoding, runner integration, trace projection, and CLI publication.
findings.next_extraction: Move trace artifact types and deterministic projection into a library module, leaving main.rs as command orchestration.

findings.reason: The compact Cairo proof codec contains the complete legacy wire schema and validation implementation in one module.
findings.next_extraction: Separate schema types, bounded decoding, and encoding while preserving byte-for-byte fixtures and rejection behavior.

findings.reason: The Rust Cairo verifier library combines oracle pinning, proof adaptation, verification, and diagnostic publication.
findings.next_extraction: Move the next stable adaptation or diagnostic boundary into a private module without weakening pinned-oracle provenance.

findings.reason: The Rust interoperability command combines multiple proof families, codecs, verification paths, and CLI reporting.
findings.next_extraction: Move shared artifact codecs and one proof-family command at a time into library modules with existing cross-language fixtures.

findings.reason: The Rust vector generator combines field, PCS, FRI, proof, and example vector families in one command root.
findings.next_extraction: Move each vector family behind a typed generator module while preserving deterministic schemas and pinned provenance.

findings.reason: The legacy arena-plan compatibility facade remains at the src root while its command families are extracted.
findings.next_extraction: Remove the root facade after all consumers use the owned src/tools/metal_arena_plan entry point.

findings.reason: A legacy RISC-V benchmark executable remains in the src root instead of its responsibility directory.
findings.next_extraction: Move the executable root under src/tools/riscv during the RISC-V migration and preserve its installed command contract.

findings.reason: A legacy RISC-V Metal benchmark executable remains in the src root instead of its responsibility directory.
findings.next_extraction: Move the executable root under src/tools/riscv and keep Metal selection at the integration boundary.

findings.reason: A legacy RISC-V prover test root remains mixed with library entry points in src.
findings.next_extraction: Move the test root under src/tests/riscv and preserve backend differential coverage.

findings.reason: A legacy RISC-V trace executable remains in the src root instead of its responsibility directory.
findings.next_extraction: Move the executable root under src/tools/riscv with trace artifact policy isolated from the frontend.
